title: lepidotis caroliniana
intro: Lepidotis caroliniana, also known as American Cranesbill or Wild Geranium, is a perennial plant species belonging to the geranium family. Its roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting the immune system and digestive health. Lepidotis caroliniana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for its herbal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
